course_title: Research in musculoskeletal, sports and exercise physiotherapy

learning_outcomes.description: Understandkey research methodologies in physiotherapy and health literature.
Interpret and applyresearch findings to inform clinical practice.
Understandthe role of consumers and the public in research design and translation.
Recognisethe benefits of quality improvement projects in health services.
Evaluateresearch quality using contemporary methodological frameworks.
Conducta research project demonstrating effective project management and data analysis.
Createan original research manuscript suitable for scientific publication.

assessments.task_description:
Students’ knowledge and understanding of the webcast content and associated readings will be assessed. Students will be given a question and provided with 10 minutes to prepare a response. During the oral examination, students will have 7 minutes to answer the question followed by 3 minutes of questions by the examiner.

This assessment task is to be completed online.

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. (Assessment Procedure (95)).

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students will critically appraise a clinical trial research article of their choice and submit their appraisal as a written assignment. Students will be guided in the use of PEDRO as the tool for the assignment, since it is designed specifically to critically appraise physiotherapy trials. The Webcast provided in Week 2 will also provide guidance for the completion of this critical appraisal.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via Turnitin as instructed in Blackboard
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Each student will conduct a formal Case Report during the semester using a patient in their current caseload. The Case Report will follow a formal structure including relevant literature review, formulation of a research question, collection of appropriate outcome measures, analysis of the measures, and interpretation of findings. 

This will be presented as a written Case Report formatted for a journal submission. Article length must be 2000 words or less.

The Case Report will be written according to JOSPT Cases Instructions to Authors and the CARE guidelines as are provided to students:

- JOSPT Cases Instructions to Authors

- CARE Case Report Guidelines (https://www.care-statement.org/)

- CARE Checklist — CARE Case Report Guidelines (https://www.care-statement.org/checklist)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 10(27 Jul - 11 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Webcasts regarding research design and translation to practice

Students will watch a series of webcasts describing different research methodologies (e.g., quantitative, qualitative, clinical trials, etc) with the intent of better informing their interpretation and application of research literature to practice. Students will be assessed on their learning/knowledge from the webcasts.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Webcasts regarding research design and translation to practice
learning_activities.topic_description: Webcasts regarding research design and translation to practice Students will watch a series of webcasts describing different research methodologies (e.g., quantitative, qualitative, clinical trials, etc) with the intent of better informing their interpretation and application of research literature to practice. Students will be assessed on their learning/knowledge from the webcasts.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Experience in conducting research

Students will gain experience in conducting research through the undertaking of a Case Report using a patient in their current clinical caseload. In preparation students will watch a specific webcast in Week 1 of Semester with associated readings which will permit them to use appropriate frameworks to develop the Case Report. This formal structure will include a relevant literature review, formulation of a research question, collection of appropriate outcome measures, analysis of the measures, and interpretation of findings. Students will present this as a written Case Report in a format appropriate for journal submission. 


Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_description: Experience in conducting research Students will gain experience in conducting research through the undertaking of a Case Report using a patient in their current clinical caseload. In preparation students will watch a specific webcast in Week 1 of Semester with associated readings which will permit them to use appropriate frameworks to develop the Case Report. This formal structure will include a relevant literature review, formulation of a research question, collection of appropriate outcome measures, analysis of the measures, and interpretation of findings. Students will present this as a written Case Report in a format appropriate for journal submission.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 8(03 Aug - 20 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Development of research critical appraisal skills

Critical appraisal skills are vital for clinicians to systematically evaluate the trustworthiness, relevance, and results of published literature. In this component of the course, students will watch a specific webcast with associated readings which will permit them to use appropriate frameworks to critically appraise research articles. They will be assessed on this learning activity.


Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
